programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britains oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyds building in the City of London

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Businesswoman and Dragons Den investor Sara Davies explores the Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire. Along the way Sara reveals the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman, the unexpected impact of participating in Strictly Come Dancing and the importance of family support

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against Joyce Grenfell, Paul Jennings and Robin Ray. With guest musician Gerald Moore. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypts most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Visits to historic places of work, including one of Britains oldest dockyards in Chatham Kent, and Armley Mill in Leeds. At a 1,000-year-old tithe barn in Sussex, the steeplejack tries his hand at thatching, before getting a surprise on the top floor of the architecturally esteemed Lloyds building in the City of London

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Businesswoman and Dragons Den investor Sara Davies explores the Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire. Along the way Sara reveals the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman, the unexpected impact of participating in Strictly Come Dancing and the importance of family support

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is appalled when she spots a strange man wrapped in only a towel exiting Elizabeths house next door. Convinced that it is her moral duty to save her friend from this scandal, Hyacinth begins to plot ways of flushing the man out

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.sub-title: Does Your Mother Know Youre Out
programme.desc: Mother wants Timothy to paint the cupboard under the stairs before the man comes to read the meter. However, Timothy is more concerned with betting snide colleague Victor that he can take new library assistant Caroline for a cinema trip to see Gone With the Wind

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian reflects on the impact of the Black Death, a disease that ravaged 14th-century England and wiped out half the population, leaving decades of anarchy and civil unrest for the survivors. The plague, which sowed the seeds of the Peasants Revolt, ironically contributed to the rise of the country gentleman

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Exploring a three-way relationship and a radically different approach to fatherhood. In Norway, trans people were subjected to forced sterilisation between 1979 and 2016. Kris, who has has always wanted to be a father, refuses to let that injustice define his future. Alongside Sindre, David and their extended families, Kris embarks on a journey that challenges traditional models of family and parenthood

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Filmed without voiceover or music, this programme highlights the call of the birds at sunrise, as those living within Britains habitats burst into song and reveal the natural splendour of their distinctive chorus

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack explores the achievements of Britains civil engineering pioneers, visiting the Channel Tunnel, Humber Bridge and Pontcysyllte Aqueduct. He also has a go at legging a narrowboat through a canal tunnel in Dudley in the West Midlands

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Nina Wadia unwinds in the spring setting of Swaledale in the Yorkshire Dales National Park, where she reflects on personal family challenges, including the loss of her parents. Nina also meets an ornithologist who shows her some of the rarer bird species found in the area, and tests her skills in a new language when bumping into a village elder who speaks Swardle, the historic local dialect

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Exploring a three-way relationship and a radically different approach to fatherhood. In Norway, trans people were subjected to forced sterilisation between 1979 and 2016. Kris, who has has always wanted to be a father, refuses to let that injustice define his future. Alongside Sindre, David and their extended families, Kris embarks on a journey that challenges traditional models of family and parenthood

programme.title: Pompeii: The Mystery of the People Frozen in Time
programme.desc: Documentary in which Margaret Mountford visits the Roman town of Pompeii, where in 79AD the catastrophic eruption of Vesuvius encased its citizens in lava, creating body casts that populate the ruins. No one has yet been able to fully explain how these people became frozen in time, so while forensic scientists peer beneath the plaster to rebuild the faces of two victims and recreate the final moments of the townsfolk, Margaret dispels the myths surrounding the events in 79AD and explores the lives of the individuals who lived in the enigmatic city

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack explores the achievements of Britains civil engineering pioneers, visiting the Channel Tunnel, Humber Bridge and Pontcysyllte Aqueduct. He also has a go at legging a narrowboat through a canal tunnel in Dudley in the West Midlands

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Nina Wadia unwinds in the spring setting of Swaledale in the Yorkshire Dales National Park, where she reflects on personal family challenges, including the loss of her parents. Nina also meets an ornithologist who shows her some of the rarer bird species found in the area, and tests her skills in a new language when bumping into a village elder who speaks Swardle, the historic local dialect

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in

programme.title: Chauvet: Humanitys First Great Masterpiece
programme.desc: Following a 30 year archaeological, geological and artistic adventure as scientists endeavour to understand and conserve the art of the Chauvet cave. Hidden deep inside Frances Gorges de lArdèche, the cave was revealed to the modern world for the very first time in 1994. It has over 1,000 paintings adorning its walls and scientists immediately recognised the importance of the discovery

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Miles urges Delilah to have an HIV test and Egg, deeply embarrassed at having caused a scene, seeks advice from Warrens therapist. Anna worries she isnt getting enough work at the chambers and hits on the bright idea of using her feminine charms to drum up business

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna organises a special dinner to fan the flickering flame of romance between Milly and Egg, while Miles gets short shrift from his father. Meanwhile, Warren fears blabbermouth cousin Kira will discover hes gay and spill the beans to the rest of the family. Jason Hughes, Jack Davenport and Daniela Nardini star

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling

programme.title: The Truth About Len Deighton
programme.desc: An interview with the writer in which he talks about his many ventures, including espionage novels, cookbooks, military histories and the films which he has written and produced. With contributions by Michael Caine and Max Hastings. First broadcast in 2006

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in

programme.title: Catching Historys Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle explore Longleat Safari Park, meeting a host of exotic, fascinating animals and the people who look after them

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms and stealing high-tech GPS systems worth hundreds of thousands of pounds at farms across the north of England. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates on a family run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying vital winter feed. As the flames spread, farmer Blaise and daughter Millie race to save their livestock. In Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus, as she is reunited with the air ambulance team members who saved her life after a brutal farm accident

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught

programme.desc: After Conor and Jock are almost caught by Sergeant Healy following a disastrous bike theft, Mairead tires of the duos immature behaviour and demands change. A road trip is duly arranged, leading to bizarre encounters with a bed and breakfast owner, the therapist herself (Father Teds Pauline McLynn), and an unsuspecting pub crowd. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The school debs is on the horizon, and Conor and Jock are looking forward to making it a great night for the girls. With Principal Walsh intent on getting in their way, the lads are determined to overcome the obstacles and make it a night to remember

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Theres a commotion in the housing estate, and Conor and Jock discover can-trailers - tearaways from other estates with open paint cans tied around bikes intent on destroying the rival estate. The lads stop their rivals, but the encounter leads to an argument and Jock is challenged to a boxing match - which he reluctantly agrees to. The duo soon discover his prospective opponent is a champion fighter, meaning Jock has to embark on an extensive training programme in order to sharpen his skills for the bout. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide its time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when theyre living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as hes about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Romania. Landing in Bucharest, the only place in the country hes heard of, Romesh meets political activist and media expert Angela-Mihaela Serban at the parliament palace, the architectural behemoth built on the command of the communist dictator Nicolai Ceausescu. Failing to warn him about the miles theyre about to clock, she takes Rom out of the capital to explore many amazing things that are overlooked in this vast slice of Eastern Europe, and the pair embark on a journey through the countrys seemingly endless and varied landscapes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A look at life after dark on the North York Moors. At the Dark Skies Festival, Margherita Taylor finds out how light pollution is affecting nocturnal wildlife, while Sammi Kinghorn meets the people gathering vital dark-sky data and installing wildlife-friendly lighting to help protect the night. Plus, Adam Henson meets apprentices at a cattle mart meeting

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the immediate fallout from the kidnap of John Cantlie and James Foley, as a perilous manhunt was launched by an expert hostage negotiator. But as the country collapsed, Islamic State took hold - and as more journalists joined them in captivity, survival became a desperate struggle

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: When Britain and the US refuse to negotiate with Islamic State, James Foley pays the ultimate price at the hands of Jihadi John. After the execution is streamed worldwide, John Cantlie reappears in a very different propaganda video. He is alive and apparently reporting on behalf of the extremists, forcing the world to question what side he is on and how far he must go to stay alive

He uncovers the secrets of the primates success - their survival tactics and physical adaptations - and how their lives mirror those of their human cousins. He begins by meeting an orang-utan that uses soap to improve its personal hygiene, baboons with a taste for flamingos, criminal-minded macaques and fluorescent mandrills that don warpaint before going into battle

Dom Chinea meets Shetlander Victoria, who needs her grandfathers accordion fixed if shes to realise her busking ambitions, and then discovers the centuries-old art of enamelling watch dials, a craft that demands steady hands

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in Cornwall, helping a couple to trade in city life for a coastal home. He also stops by a regenerative farm focused on nourishing land, body and mind

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back four finalist contenders to take on the Gladiators, and each other, for the title of champion in the grand final. Viper leaves the Gladiators gobsmacked, Bradley races Electro on the track, and much to Guy Mowbrays surprise, Legend tries his hand at commentary

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: The newsreader heads to the medieval Tuscan town of Barga, where more than half the locals have Scottish connections. Staying in a mansion thats now a Glaswegian-run BandB, Clive finds out why Italians love a fish supper, helps cook dinner for 14 and joins an expedition to discover the forgotten quartz quarry that helped make Tuscanys capital Florence so beautiful

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: Zoologist George McGavin continues his exploration of the primate world, considering how the secret to their success is the ability to live in close-knit family and social groups. This is demonstrated by the emperor tamarin males conned into childcare in Peru and the South African vervet monkeys that have a sophisticated alarm system to protect them from predators. But living together often has its problems, like the Barbary macaques in Gibraltar who have their babies stolen by others out to impress their boss, the matriarchal ring-tailed lemur societies where females battle one another with their children in tow, and the hamadryas baboons of Ethiopia where the males rule with an iron fist

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts visit Eastnor Castle in Herefordshire, where they value a wooden bicycle, an embroidered egg and the first All Blacks rugby shirt. Also among the treasures are a large pocket knife with 96 implements and a First World War pack with a bullet still lodged in it. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds out about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Ahir Shah, Michaela Strachan, Dr Xand van Tulleken and Vogue Williams test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Northern Ireland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The judges are 2025s banquet winners Sally Abe and Jean Delport. Dishes include a red knotted brioche handkerchief as a homage to School for Good and Evil. Also, theres a tribute to director Lisa Barros DSa with barbecued monkfish served from a teapot

programme.title: Live UEFA Womens Champions League
programme.desc: Chelsea v Arsenal (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the quarter-final second-leg tie, held at Stamford Bridge, as the sides contest a place in the last four against VfL Wolfsburg or Lyon. Arsenal are the holders of this competition, while Chelsea have never lifted the trophy, but will feel success in this competition is long over-due, having dominated the domestic game in recent years and reached the semi-finals in each of the previous three campaigns

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan continues his journey along Irelands Wild Atlantic coast with an emotional trip with his nephew to Clare Island, a special place for the entire Keating family. They get to grips with a traditional loom before Ronan heads for Sligo to meet up with a fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, for some horse riding on the golden sands of Strandhill Beach

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, with Katie Razzall

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Captive Audience: A Real American Horror Story
programme.desc: Featuring a heroic escape and a televised family reunion. However, the Stayner ordeal is far from over. When a hit TV movie rewrote the Stayner story, reality got stranger than fiction

programme.title: Belfast (2021)
programme.desc: Surrounded by sporadic violence and growing danger, nine-year-old Buddy finds himself confronted with the ugly reality of sectarian conflict in 1960s Belfast. As the suffocating stranglehold of increasing turmoil tightens around his once-peaceful working-class neighbourhood, Buddy tries his best to understand the Troubles. Kenneth Branaghs drama, starring Jamie Dornan, Judi Dench and Jude Hill

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: Documentary looking at four of Britains 15 national parks, capturing the sights and sounds of these unique places and meeting the people who live and work there. The opening edition visits the New Forest, once a royal hunting ground for William the Conqueror and now a mosaic of open heathlands, ancient woodlands, coastline and picturesque villages. The park is home to ponies, deer, rare bird species and reptiles, while the traditional grazing practices of generations of commoners sustain this landscape, with their ponies and pigs roaming freely throughout the park

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from Northern Ireland are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. programme.title: Sphinx
programme.desc: Dutch mystery thriller about a teenager who reappears after having been missing for years, the only clue to what has happened a tattoo on her back. She remains unaware that her kidnapper has allowed her to escape, and blinded her trauma, she fulfils her role in his plan. Starring Rifka Lodeizen. In Dutch and French

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Emmas new shop is flourishing - and so is romance with the handsome David. But love has let her down before, and she finds herself torn between following her heart and revenge on the Fairleys. Meanwhile, a newly sober Adele crashes back into Fairley Hall, jeopardising Adam and Olivias affair and Edwins upcoming marriage to Priya. In the 1970s, Emma commands her granddaughter Paula to break off her romance with Jim Fairley, forcing Paula to choose between her grandmother and her lover

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: At Fairley Hall, death has left a void. Grief-stricken Gerald is in serious debt to a mysterious lender, with the Fairley mill tied up as collateral. Emma has settled into her marriage to Joe Lowther and works tirelessly to be a brilliant wife and mother but theres tension in the household; Joe knows her work will always come first. While Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means theyve got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at a loft conversion in Walthamstow, east London, for the Clayton-Fisher family, who wanted to transform their dark and dingy attic. Sarah and her wife Pepper were struggling for space in their two-bed terrace and with plans to start a family on hold, Sarah thought it was time to move on, but Pepper dug her heels in and wanted to convert the loft

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Mercedes is determined to find out what Jensons up to, Ste makes a big decision, and a sister is backed into a corner. Warren decides who he wants, but is he too late? Friends gather around to support Diane and Tony, Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image. At The Loft relaunch party, a shocking turn of events puts all eyes on Mercedes

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sams friend Rick falls on hard times so the bar owner reluctantly lends him his lucky charm - then hits a losing streak himself. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla tricks a delighted Marshall into believing he is the father of her unborn baby, much to Dianes disapproval. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: The Spy Who Came in for a Cold One
programme.desc: Diane incurs the wrath of Sam and the entire barroom clan when she exposes a customer who spouts captivating, tall tales of his exploits as a Second World War spy. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Ray end up with the mother of all guilt trips when they decide not to help Marie with a home project so they can land a prime spot on the golf course

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank and Marie announce their plans to move into a retirement home in New Jersey - much to the relief of their family. While Robert is overjoyed to be getting his hands on his parents house at last, Debra is surprised to find she has misgivings about losing touch with her in-laws

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray visit Frank and Marie, only to learn they are facing eviction from their new home - so Robert is forced to let them move back in with him. American comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles girlfriend Mel persuades him to compete against Frasier for the honour of becoming cork master at a local wine club, causing friction in the Crane household. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin helps trap an unfaithful husband, while Roz and Frasier attend a conference on behalf of the radio station, and find themselves with no choice but to share a room. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink lands a job as guest host on a morning TV show, and finds himself considering a permanent move from radio. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: To Thine Old Self Be True
programme.desc: Niles finds out that Maris has put on weight while pining for him, and Frasier throws a bachelor party for Donny - but ends up handcuffed to a stripper. Starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part edition, Gordon Ramsay heads to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, an Italian restaurant run by sisters Rita and Lisa. The pair grew up working at their fathers eatery nearby, but when they opened their own business, they couldnt replicate his success, and they now blame their failure on everything from the weather to the tablecloths

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4s midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a gramophone and a stow-away bed to restore. Meanwhile, Simon accidently puts his foot through a drum

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: As snow falls, Matt sets out to transform an old barn into a poultry shed to protect Janices prize-winning rare-breed chickens from the cold months ahead. And in the wake of the storm, attention turns to making safe an old ash tree positioned perilously close to the farmhouse with the help of tree surgeons Oli and Alex

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Mike Bubbins joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe goes house-hunting in the southern French city of Carcassonne with Sue and Tony, in search of their perfect holiday bolthole for their budget of £100,000. This isnt the couples first viewing trip to the area, and theres already a property on their radar, but can any of the five that Craig shows them beat it?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother Nature wreaks havoc as heavy rain prevents Richard and Simons bar in Portugal from opening and brings work outside of Mark and Garys gîte in France grinding to a halt. In Italy, boiler problems at Lisa and Pauls holiday apartment bring disarray to their first booking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Ami and Mark host the weeks second visit at Kits Coty Glamping near Aylesford in Kent. Some of the BandBers are hailing their huts at the plush glampsite - but alarm bells are ringing for Emma Louise and Liv in their bell tent. After a history tour in the afternoon, its the hosts who are wanting to consign some cleanliness comments to the past

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On night two in Sheffield, law lecturer Sally serves up an authentic old school Shanghai menu. The group all dress to impress but Ambrose is worried that Sally might be his biggest competition and has got a sweat on. He tries to contain his glee when he finds a bit of twine in his starter but is otherwise impressed by her presentation and flavours. Everyone sees a new side to Sally when she reveals an enormous tattoo that runs down her leg and her talents seem to have no end as she showcases her piano skills on the spinet

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: The enormity of the project hits home hard as Jasmine and Jon begin renovations on their Costa del Sol home. They enlist local builder Carlos to make the property as habitable as possible before the family move in, while Jasmine seeks inspiration by visiting a friend who is approaching the end of their build

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A Great Dane called Scooby comes into Woodgreen looking for a new friend to take home and the Davies family have a double date with Moana the Labrador and Buffy the German shepherd puppy. Fox the stray pomeranian has a second chance at adoption

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britains most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. After 40 years living largely in the southeast of England, artist Sarah is returning to her roots in the northeast with towering ambitions for an exposed, three-acre plot that shes bought at auction in the Durham Dales. She wants to create a radical home and creative hub combined, including a studio and gallery for displaying and selling art. With her budget of £330,000, Sarah hopes to build with a super-sustainable yet still relatively unknown and hard-to-predict material - hempcrete. Left exposed throughout the interior, the hempcrete will provide a dramatic earthy look, but this relies on the building process going perfectly. But, just a few months into Sarahs eight-month schedule, her plan to move in before winter is scuppered as her inexperience as a project manager starts to show. With material costs going through the roof, and her budget doing likewise, selling her one-bed holiday cottage looks like the only way to keep building. As the critical hemp process gets going, and proves increasingly tortuous and unpredictable, the success of Sarahs project hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Seven players remain, all vying to win up to £100,000. But with five Predators and only two Prey, the competition must undergo a reset. As the rules of the game take a radical new direction, theres a brutal surprise for one player and a dramatic reversal of fortune for another. But its an agonising, heart-breaking decision forced onto one Prey that provides the biggest upset of the game so far

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions of shows including Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Race Across the World, The Piano, I Kissed a Boy, Made in Chelsea, Prue Leiths Cotswold Kitchen and a BBC Breakfast report on the then recently appointed Pope Leo XIV. From May 2025

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew fight to save their patients partially amputated hand in Stowmarket and a crew in High Wycombe to help a man in cardiac arrest. The Essex and Herts Air Ambulance team deal with Beckys badly broken ankle in Chelmsford and in Hatfield, a motorcyclist suffers multiple injuries after a collision with a van

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers settle in for The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer, Saturday Night Live UK, Britains Got Talent, The Hunt: Prey vs Predator, Celebrity Sabotage, Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen, The Dog House and BBC News on Easter egg inflation

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Jess and Ben reveal a love of nudism and a desire to make the transition from the naturist scene to a non-monogamous lifestyle. However, Jesss hatred of social situations must first be conquered. Elsewhere, Kirsty and Tom have the opportunity to take a giant leap in opening their relationship by having bisexual experiences with the retreats residents and consider attending a huge orgy

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: The Predators band together and ruthlessly round on one player, whose survival hangs in the balance. Meanwhile, one of the contests closest alliances is put in peril. There is an unexpected change of fortune, as one player goes from underdog to top dog. After one of the toughest hunts yet, the players return to base, where politics and power plays kick in, before another of the group is culled from the contest

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by Nabil Abdulrashid and Charlotte Church for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, compete to host the best dinner party, and win a £1,000 prize. The contestants taking part are fine-dining enthusiasts Dan and Jacqui, lovebirds Hannah and Lucy, and final pair Edyta and Simon - who treat their guests to an international menu

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The architect is in Stratford-upon-Avon, where he helps Laura and Jonnie to transform their Edwardian mock Tudor house into a modern family home. George also meets Nicki, whose 1920s cottage in Essex needs major changes to make it more accessible

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Mike Bubbins joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A TV agent visits the bar and offers Sam a contract doing beer commercials - but soon makes it clear she doesnt want their relationship to be purely professional. Ted Danson stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Preoccupied with the ball game on TV, nobody pays much attention to Dianes sadness over the death of her family cat. It is left to Sam to discover the reason for Dianes grief. Meanwhile, Sam and Coach use insider information to make a bet

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane is aghast when Carla tricks the surprised but ecstatic Marshall Lipton into believing he is the father of her unborn child. Bar-room comedy, starring Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray realises that the angrier Debra gets, the better their sex life becomes - so he decides to stir up conflict between her and Marie. American comedy, starring Patricia Heaton, Doris Roberts and Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Members of the parent-teacher association complain about Debras outfits, leaving Ray facing the delicate task of persuading her to dress more conservatively in future. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally comes home with a low grade on her report card. Ray is inclined to blame the teacher, but it turns out there is another reason why she is feeling distracted in class. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star, with Madylin Sweeten

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A classy restaurant awards the shrink a place on its wall of fame, but he takes exception to the caricature of himself and tries to persuade the head waiter to replace it with a more flattering likeness. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphnes brother turns up at the wedding, bringing nothing with him but an unquenchable thirst for alcohol and a squadron of drinking pals. Meanwhile, the bride finally becomes aware of Niless feelings for her. Comedy, guest starring Anthony LaPaglia, with David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Something Borrowed, Someone Blue - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. The shrink proffers romantic advice to both Niles and Daphne, hoping that they will admit their mutual affection. However, it remains to be seen whether they will have the nerve to be honest with each other before her impending marriage to Donny

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Something Borrowed, Someone Blue - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Daphne prepares to tie the knot with Donny, her family arrives for the wedding of the year and Niles struggles with his feelings for her

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the conclusion of Gordon Ramsays visit to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, he confronts sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum, and finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the Italian restaurants failings

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4s midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole decides that he needs to restore a forklift truck, while Simon OBrien gets all zen with a stone water feature

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Its lambing season and the family are eagerly awaiting the arrival of the first black Welsh mountain sheep to be born on the farm. Matt begins work on his dream woodland hut and wildlife hideaway and Nicola, Luke and Molly do their best to make the pond area more accessible for Mike and Janice

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Mike Bubbins joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Alison from south Staffordshire are seeking a rural holiday home in the Haute-Vienne area of central France. For their budget of £100,000, expert Laura Hamilton lines up five properties, but will any of them prove suitable enough to prompt the pair into making an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, Mark and Gary find out if their gite will be awarded the coveted five-star rating and in Portugal, there are kitchen problems and toilet troubles at Richard and Simons bar on their busy opening weekend. In Italy, missing paperwork prevents Lisa and Paul from opening their newly renovated apartment and Becky and Gav have a mountain to climb to complete their new home and holiday lets in the Pyrenees

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its been a week packed full of history in Kent, and thats no different at Sabans 700-year-old Stone Court House in Maidstone. The heritage character of his place is an instant hit, and the BandBers get some much-needed bonding done during a trip along the River Medway. But the smooth waters dont last long, as evening drinks turn terrifically tense

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third host in Sheffield, Leon, is hoping to inject some spice into the competition with a scorching Caribbean supper. But when Leon dishes up a fiery main course, a few nervous stomachs rumble. Leon keeps himself in the spotlight with some impromptu warbling, but will his guests join in or struggle to hide their awkward discomfort? Hes soon in hot water, as he struggles to place names to faces despite being three nights in

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine gets hands-on as the patio is dug up and the family bathroom is demolished. Liliana Myers, widow of Hairy Biker Dave Myers, drops in to see how her friend Jon has settled into his new life, and all seems to be going well - until a medical emergency puts everything on hold

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: In Nottinghamshire, Laura has snapped up a 100-year-old abandoned bungalow with ivy growing under the wallpaper. Mortgage-free in her mid-thirties, shes determined to create her dream home. But funds are tight, so she has to roll up her sleeves and tackle much of the renovation herself. In Stoke-on-Trent, Tariq has taken ownership of a local landmark - the former Minton Library. To fund the restoration of its historic tiles, he plans to transform the upper floors into an events venue. The pressures on, as he has just eight weeks until he hosts his first wedding, but spiralling costs and looming deadlines threaten to derail everything. Plus, behind the scenes at the East Anglia Auction House where a tiny plot of land sparks a bidding frenzy, and the new owners of what locals call Norfolks creepiest house face a big decision - demolish or restore?

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Britain is at war, and Emma frantically searches for a way to keep her businesses afloat. She has an ingenious plan, but its a huge gamble. Meanwhile, the men in Emmas life decide whether to sign up and risk their lives for their country. When a guilt-ridden Edwin enlists, Adam is willing to do anything to stop him, even prising open the past. In the 1970s, Paula and Emma are reunited as Paula rushes to warn her grandmother of a surprise twist in the coup against her

programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Great Diamond Heist
programme.desc: In 2015, a gang of ageing criminals carried out one of the most audacious heists in British history, stealing a multi-million-pound haul of jewellery and gold from a high-security vault in Londons Hatton Garden diamond district. More than a decade after the heist, what happened to the gang, and what happened to the missing millions? Insiders, including one of the crew who carried out the heist, reveal the extraordinary twists, turns and betrayals which followed the heist and eventually led to the gangs capture

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Andy, 36, is brought in after a motorcycle accident with a fractured femur that needs realignment. Staff fear 27-year-old Connor, who has fallen off a roof, may have bleeding on his brain. In the urgent treatment unit, 25-year-old Adrian has suffered injuries to his face and been bitten on his arm after breaking up a fight in the city centre

programme.title: Britains Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Broker Constance is on a mission to get the listing for Laura Comforts £12.5million home as the celebrity singer wants to downsize to a £4million pad in Chelsea. Managing director Guy values a quaint Tudor cottage in Henley on Thames - a little corner of paradise that owner Brian is selling for the not insignificant sum of £1.75million. It boasts a swimming pool and a shower in a former phone box

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay heads to Windsor, Vermont, on a mission to boost the fortunes of the Juniper Hill Inn, which may look impressive, but smells of raw sewage owing to a plumbing problem. After meeting the BandBs wealthy owner Robert Dean II, the chef uncovers some shocking treatment toward staff and realises he will need something approaching a miracle to save this business from disaster

programme.title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
programme.desc: Thanks to his race car driver owner, golden retriever Enzo learns that the techniques needed on the racetrack can also be used to successfully navigate the journey of life. Comedy drama, starring Milo Ventimiglia and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three Essex couples compete to host the best dinner party and win a cash prize. Asset manager Amie and husband Mark got married one year previously, and Amie believes she is in charge of both the relationship and the kitchen. Teacher Nathan unleashes some home truths about his partner - veterinary nurse Gemma - while Simon and Suzanne find themselves under pressure when things heat up in the kitchen

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers settle in for The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer, Saturday Night Live UK, Britains Got Talent, The Hunt: Prey vs Predator, Celebrity Sabotage, Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen, The Dog House and BBC News on Easter egg inflation

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Jess and Ben reveal a love of nudism and a desire to make the transition from the naturist scene to a non-monogamous lifestyle. However, Jesss hatred of social situations must first be conquered. Elsewhere, Kirsty and Tom have the opportunity to take a giant leap in opening their relationship by having bisexual experiences with the retreats residents and consider attending a huge orgy

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Emmas new shop is flourishing - and so is romance with the handsome David. But love has let her down before, and she finds herself torn between following her heart and revenge on the Fairleys. Meanwhile, a newly sober Adele crashes back into Fairley Hall, jeopardising Adam and Olivias affair and Edwins upcoming marriage to Priya. In the 1970s, Emma commands her granddaughter Paula to break off her romance with Jim Fairley, forcing Paula to choose between her grandmother and her lover

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: At Fairley Hall, death has left a void. Grief-stricken Gerald is in serious debt to a mysterious lender, with the Fairley mill tied up as collateral. Emma has settled into her marriage to Joe Lowther and works tirelessly to be a brilliant wife and mother but theres tension in the household; Joe knows her work will always come first. While Emma prepares the perfect Christmas for her family, her unrelenting obsession with work and revenge brings danger to her door. In the 1970s, as Emma recovers from a heart attack, an oblivious Paula doubles down on her love affair with Jim

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In the seaside town of Weymouth, Dorset, the presenter helps a couple whose first-time family home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood. When they bought their 19th-century coach house in 2018, they had planned to renovate its higgledy-piggledy layout, but the overnight disaster means theyve got a far bigger project than they bargained for. The architect also visits Chiswick, west London, to assist a couple whose master bedroom and bathroom he redesigned a couple of years ago. With a growing family, next on their demolition list is the pokey kitchen

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at a loft conversion in Walthamstow, east London, for the Clayton-Fisher family, who wanted to transform their dark and dingy attic. Sarah and her wife Pepper were struggling for space in their two-bed terrace and with plans to start a family on hold, Sarah thought it was time to move on, but Pepper dug her heels in and wanted to convert the loft

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Mercedes is determined to find out what Jensons up to, Ste makes a big decision, and a sister is backed into a corner. Warren decides who he wants, but is he too late? Friends gather around to support Diane and Tony, Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image. At The Loft relaunch party, a shocking turn of events puts all eyes on Mercedes

programme.desc: Carla tricks a delighted Marshall into believing he is the father of her unborn baby, much to Dianes disapproval. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Rhea Perlman

programme.desc: Diane incurs the wrath of Sam and the entire barroom clan when she exposes a customer who spouts captivating, tall tales of his exploits as a Second World War spy. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert and Ray end up with the mother of all guilt trips when they decide not to help Marie with a home project so they can land a prime spot on the golf course

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray visit Frank and Marie, only to learn they are facing eviction from their new home - so Robert is forced to let them move back in with him. American comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.desc: Martin helps trap an unfaithful husband, while Roz and Frasier attend a conference on behalf of the radio station, and find themselves with no choice but to share a room. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

The pair grew up working at their fathers eatery nearby, but when they opened their own business, they couldnt replicate his success, and they now blame their failure on everything from the weather to the tablecloths

And in the wake of the storm, attention turns to making safe an old ash tree positioned perilously close to the farmhouse with the help of tree surgeons Oli and Alex

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with Mike Bubbins joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.desc: Craig Rowe goes house-hunting in the southern French city of Carcassonne with Sue and Tony, in search of their perfect holiday bolthole for their budget of £100,000. This isnt the couples first viewing trip to the area, and theres already a property on their radar, but can any of the five that Craig shows them beat it?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother Nature wreaks havoc as heavy rain prevents Richard and Simons bar in Portugal from opening and brings work outside of Mark and Garys gîte in France grinding to a halt. In Italy, boiler problems at Lisa and Pauls holiday apartment bring disarray to their first booking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Married couple Ami and Mark host the weeks second visit at Kits Coty Glamping near Aylesford in Kent. Some of the BandBers are hailing their huts at the plush glampsite - but alarm bells are ringing for Emma Louise and Liv in their bell tent. After a history tour in the afternoon, its the hosts who are wanting to consign some cleanliness comments to the past

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On night two in Sheffield, law lecturer Sally serves up an authentic old school Shanghai menu. The group all dress to impress but Ambrose is worried that Sally might be his biggest competition and has got a sweat on. He tries to contain his glee when he finds a bit of twine in his starter but is otherwise impressed by her presentation and flavours. Everyone sees a new side to Sally when she reveals an enormous tattoo that runs down her leg and her talents seem to have no end as she showcases her piano skills on the spinet

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: The enormity of the project hits home hard as Jasmine and Jon begin renovations on their Costa del Sol home. programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A Great Dane called Scooby comes into Woodgreen looking for a new friend to take home and the Davies family have a double date with Moana the Labrador and Buffy the German shepherd puppy. Fox the stray pomeranian has a second chance at adoption

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britains most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. After 40 years living largely in the southeast of England, artist Sarah is returning to her roots in the northeast with towering ambitions for an exposed, three-acre plot that shes bought at auction in the Durham Dales. She wants to create a radical home and creative hub combined, including a studio and gallery for displaying and selling art. With her budget of £330,000, Sarah hopes to build with a super-sustainable yet still relatively unknown and hard-to-predict material - hempcrete. Left exposed throughout the interior, the hempcrete will provide a dramatic earthy look, but this relies on the building process going perfectly. But, just a few months into Sarahs eight-month schedule, her plan to move in before winter is scuppered as her inexperience as a project manager starts to show. With material costs going through the roof, and her budget doing likewise, selling her one-bed holiday cottage looks like the only way to keep building. As the critical hemp process gets going, and proves increasingly tortuous and unpredictable, the success of Sarahs project hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Seven players remain, all vying to win up to £100,000. But with five Predators and only two Prey, the competition must undergo a reset. As the rules of the game take a radical new direction, theres a brutal surprise for one player and a dramatic reversal of fortune for another. But its an agonising, heart-breaking decision forced onto one Prey that provides the biggest upset of the game so far

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: In Nottinghamshire, Laura has snapped up a 100-year-old abandoned bungalow with ivy growing under the wallpaper. Mortgage-free in her mid-thirties, shes determined to create her dream home. But funds are tight, so she has to roll up her sleeves and tackle much of the renovation herself. In Stoke-on-Trent, Tariq has taken ownership of a local landmark - the former Minton Library. To fund the restoration of its historic tiles, he plans to transform the upper floors into an events venue. The pressures on, as he has just eight weeks until he hosts his first wedding, but spiralling costs and looming deadlines threaten to derail everything. Plus, behind the scenes at the East Anglia Auction House where a tiny plot of land sparks a bidding frenzy, and the new owners of what locals call Norfolks creepiest house face a big decision - demolish or restore?

programme.title: A Woman of Substance
programme.desc: Britain is at war, and Emma frantically searches for a way to keep her businesses afloat. She has an ingenious plan, but its a huge gamble. Meanwhile, the men in Emmas life decide whether to sign up and risk their lives for their country. When a guilt-ridden Edwin enlists, Adam is willing to do anything to stop him, even prising open the past. programme.title: Hatton Garden: The Great Diamond Heist
programme.desc: In 2015, a gang of ageing criminals carried out one of the most audacious heists in British history, stealing a multi-million-pound haul of jewellery and gold from a high-security vault in Londons Hatton Garden diamond district. More than a decade after the heist, what happened to the gang, and what happened to the missing millions? Insiders, including one of the crew who carried out the heist, reveal the extraordinary twists, turns and betrayals which followed the heist and eventually led to the gangs capture

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics

programme.desc: How manufacturer Lea and Perrins concocts its famous Worcestershire sauce. Plus, the shredding of cars and the making of sarees

programme.title: Motor Pickers

programme.title: Shed and Buried

programme.desc: Bike guru Henry Cole and ace mechanic Sam Lovegrove look around a shed full of vintage motorcycles and intriguing memorabilia. The guys see potential in a Yamaha RD350, but can they afford it?

programme.title: Planes That Changed the World

programme.desc: Steve and Sam get into deep trouble transporting a state-of-the-art Navy mine sweeper, while Simon has his hands full cleaning up after moving a 40-tonne vehicle

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson guides contestants through the game of choosing a partner naked, as they must whittle six contenders down to one with whom to share a date. Here, sick of the party lifestyle, 27-year-old former club rep Chris, from London, hopes to meet someone to settle down with. Next up is 27-year-old technophobe Louise, from Buckinghamshire, who hasnt so far embraced the modern way of dating. A fan of classic romantic novels, Louise is looking for an old fashioned gentleman to sweep her off her feet

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit as alliances fracture, one marriage ends and the experiment is left permanently shaken

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old virgin Josh hopes to find a fairy-tale princess to remove his innocence, while twentysomething Manchester couple Matt and Mary are interested in exploring the possibilities of polyamory and finding a third person to join their relationship. However, with two people doing the picking, will they ever be able to agree on who to date?

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Following their flirty chats, Kit and Inga head out on a date, leaving Jazz questioning why the news is bothering her so much. Jules brother Philip ramps up his feud with Chuggs, telling everyone - including Jules boyfriend Tristan - that Chuggs fancies Jules. Freddy also finds himself caught in the middle, with his loyalties split between Angus and Jack

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Geoff struggles to balance demands from the Jenkintown Posse and Erica. Meanwhile, Adam faces a crisis of confidence over his career path

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Valentines Day approaches, Barry is shocked when an attractive teaching assistant agrees to go out with him. Meanwhile, Adam and Dana worry that theyve missed their chance with their new crushes

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom Carl plans a Spanish wedding for his fiancee Rebecca, as he tries to arrange the perfect day to meet her expectations for a high-class affair

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image at an LGBTQ+ club night, and at the Loft relaunch party, a shocking turn of events puts all eyes on Mercedes

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Warren struggles to trust his estranged brother Jenson, Diane makes a decision as her family struggle with her prognosis and a resident is forced to reveal the truth

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos on the themes of food and animals

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family-run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now its down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michons from disaster

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Dave Kims parents out of town for the weekend, Adam decides to throw a giant party to impress Brea. Beverly lets fame go to her head after her cookbook is published

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam is thrilled when William Penn institutes a new open lunch policy, but Beverly has other plans. Geoff gets more than he bargained for when he uses Barrys student ID

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire help out with their childrens school projects, while Gloria and Cameron spend quality time together. Elsewhere, Mitchell and Manny learn they have more in common than they thought during a trip to watch a meteor shower with Jay. Comedy, starring Ty Burrell and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire struggles to get hold of the years must-have gadget for Phils birthday. Cam gets involved in a neighbours marital problems, while Mitchell decides it is time to toughen up. Comedy, starring Julie Bowen, Ty Burrell, Eric Stonestreet and Jesse Tyler Ferguson

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit as alliances fracture, one marriage ends and the experiment is left permanently shaken

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Cake-maker and self-confessed princess Fiona seeks someone chunky and funky, finding herself paired with recruitment consultant Rakim. Ex-soldier Tristan from Exeter is on a mission to find his perfect woman after being single for four years. Will beach-loving Ema get swept away by his passion for the south west? Twenty-nine-year-old Lewis - whose narcolepsy caused him to fall asleep on a previous date - is paired with DJ Thom, while lager and football-lover Jessica is introduced to fellow northerner and pub-lover Ryan

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family heads to New York after Bart hears his ex-girlfriend Mary Spuckler (Zooey Deschanel) is living there. But once he arrives, the spiky-haired youngster revisits a few more of his old flames - voiced by Anne Hathaway, Sarah Michelle Gellar, Natalie Portman and Sarah Silverman - while Lisa and Marge take in the local culture

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is horrified at Marges attempts to make him befriend Ralph Wiggum - until he learns his police chief dad has a master key to every shop in Springfield. In a bid to impress Nelson and his bully buddies, Bart and Ralph break into an abandoned prison, where they find an electric chair

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: In an episode inspired by Hitchcock thriller Strangers on a Train, Claire and Mitchell agree to be brutally honest with each others partners - so Phil is informed his stand-up comedy is cringeworthy, and Cam is told his decision to wear biker shorts is unwise. Comedy, starring Julie Bowen and Jesse Tyler Ferguson

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit as alliances fracture, one marriage ends and the experiment is left permanently shaken

programme.title: Dance Moms: A New Era
programme.desc: A fresh rivalry is born when a new dancer arrives - except shes not so new to Gina, who has a history with her new teammate - which is exacerbated when both girls are given solos

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A tornado hits Springfield and Marge and Homer have a brush with death that prompts them to think about who would look after their kids if the worst were to happen. But finding suitable guardians for their brood is no easy feat - until they bump into super-cool couple Mav and Portia. Hes a surfer and shes an environmental lawyer - but are they just too good to be true?

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Vicky Pattison: Maybe, Baby?
programme.desc: Vicky and Ercan explore more options for starting a family, meeting a mum who had her children through surrogacy and speaking to an adoptive mum as they continue searching for answers. But will these conversations help them make a decision on becoming parents, or lead them to the difficult realisation that parenthood isnt right for them?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including Wheel of Fortune, Trigger Point, Michael McIntyres Big Show, Boarders, Love Is Blind and The Pet Psychic: Whats Your Dog Thinking? They also reflect on BBC News coverage of UK by-elections and Donald Trumps bid to take the White House. From February 2024

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: More singletons meet up over a meal at the First Dates restaurant. Eccentric theology student Louisa is looking for someone to love her as she is. programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will plans to celebrate his birthday with a grown-up dinner, but his friends face a dilemma when they discover the event clashes with a more popular party. They end up gatecrashing the rival gathering, only to find that Simons French exchange student steals the limelight

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will tries to reinvent the boys image by taking them clubbing around London, but Simon is unsure whether to go until Carli shows an interest in the outing. programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old virgin Josh hopes to find a fairy-tale princess to remove his innocence, while twentysomething Manchester couple Matt and Mary are interested in exploring the possibilities of polyamory and finding a third person to join their relationship. However, with two people doing the picking, will they ever be able to agree on who to date?

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Following their flirty chats, Kit and Inga head out on a date, leaving Jazz questioning why the news is bothering her so much. programme.desc: Geoff struggles to balance demands from the Jenkintown Posse and Erica. Meanwhile, Adam faces a crisis of confidence over his career path

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The bikers explore the county of Tyne and Wear and the city of Newcastle Upon Tyne, where Simon King has spent most of his life. They cook a roast dinner - a porchetta with all the trimmings, including Yorkshire puddings and a spiced apple sauce. programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives more of the nations favourite dishes a twist, making sticky toffee pudding with his mother Sally and rustling up a mouth-watering steak and onion sandwich. The chef also prepares an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his stamp on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat - chicken Kiev, before the chef prepares spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist and a deluxe shepherds pie made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder rather than mince

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter and Tuscan mashed chickpeas for the team who built her barn

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares raspberry muffins and coffee for breakfast, before heading to local restaurant South Fork Kitchen

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve contestants begin the battle to be named Britains best amateur baker, facing tasks devised by judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who have created 30 new tasks to test their baking skills and creativity. Their first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake, before they attempt to bake a frosted walnut cake in the technical. Day two of the contest sees the contestants going all out to try and impress the judges with their first showstopper, the Black Forest gateau. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: With the cake week completed, and one of their number already eliminated from the competition, the 11 remaining home bakers must now prove their skill at making biscuits, a task that can be deceptively difficult, and one that has undone many a confident Bake Off contestant in the past. The opening signature challenge this week is to create 24 biscotti, Italian delicacies that must be baked twice to deliver the perfect crisp crunch. Next is Paul Hollywoods technical challenge, in which the contestants tackle Arlettes - labour-intensive, puff pastry-based treats from southwest France. Finally, for the showstopper, the judges demand bespoke edible biscuit boxes, filled with 36 biscuits of different flavours. After all three rounds have concluded, it falls to Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins to announce which of the bakers will be asked to leave the tent

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and his companion Tom Kitchin head to Edinburgh, but first they take a detour to a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where James knocks up whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once they reach the capital, Tom shares his knowledge of the local fish, James gets fitted for a kilt and learns the history of the iconic garment, and the pair enjoy a bite to eat at Toms Michelin-starred restaurant

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver prepares a hearty fish pie, helped by his son Buddy, with a fish curry using coconut milk and spinach for the sauce. He also makes gnudi - like gnocchi, but lighter - served with a butter and sage sauce, and hummingbird cake, an American sponge with pineapple, banana and cinnamon among its ingredients

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Popular chef Jamie Olivers latest menu features ham, egg and chips with a Jamaican jerk rub and caramelised pineapple, chicken kebab served with houmous and mango amba sauce, and a three-tiered chocolate sponge cake complete with nougat frosting

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: The chef shares her favourite recipes for effortless entertaining including chilli tortilla chips, slow roasted fillet of beef with basil and parmesan mayonnaise

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten prepares a birthday surprise for her friend, whipping up an Italian seafood salad and German chocolate cupcakes

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The 10 remaining home-bakers must steel themselves to face the full force of Paul Hollywoods discerning eye as bread week arrives. For the signature bake, the contestants are given an hour-and-a-half to prepare quick breads with no proving - which results in creative flavours such as sweet chocolate loaves and manchego and prosciutto-stuffed bread coming to the fore. Next up is the technical challenge, in which Paul lulls the bakers into a false sense of security by asking them to make baguettes, a relatively simple task that concludes with the male judge delivering one of his most brutal verdicts yet. Finally, a bicycle, a snake, a lion and even Brighton Pavilion find their way into the tents ovens as the showstopper challenge sees the competitors crafting edible 3D bread sculptures

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins usher the nine remaining home-bakers back into the tent for the fourth week of the contest. This time, the competitors must tackle desserts, beginning with a signature challenge in the form of a French classic - creme brulees. With the clock ticking, the contestants must grill their offerings to perfection to avoid serving up an unappetising scrambled egg and custard soup. Next up is the technical challenge, in which Mary Berry asks the group to prepare Spanische Windtorte, an Austrian meringue inspired by the tastes of Spain, Switzerland and France. Finally, the showstopper this week involves baking a three-tiered cheesecake stack, and only the most structurally sound creations will be worthy of securing their maker the title of star baker

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James joins up with fellow chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen to explore Whitby, in James home county of North Yorkshire. James pops into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa, meanwhile, pays a visit to Michelin-starred restaurant The Black Swan, to see how its huge kitchen garden provides almost all of the produce used in the kitchen

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen in Lancashire, where they visit a food hall and a brewery in Clitheroe, as well Lisas Michelin-starred restaurant Northcote in the village of Langho. Along the way they prepare barbecued John Dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad, Blackburn and Eccles cakes with a selection of cheeses and a classic lamb hot pot

Day two of the contest sees the contestants going all out to try and impress the judges with their first showstopper, the Black Forest gateau. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: With the cake week completed, and one of their number already eliminated from the competition, the 11 remaining home bakers must now prove their skill at making biscuits, a task that can be deceptively difficult, and one that has undone many a confident Bake Off contestant in the past. The opening signature challenge this week is to create 24 biscotti, Italian delicacies that must be baked twice to deliver the perfect crisp crunch. Next is Paul Hollywoods technical challenge, in which the contestants tackle Arlettes - labour-intensive, puff pastry-based treats from southwest France. Finally, for the showstopper, the judges demand bespoke edible biscuit boxes, filled with 36 biscuits of different flavours. After all three rounds have concluded, it falls to Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins to announce which of the bakers will be asked to leave the tent
