programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Accused of defrauding an elderly person, Tonika has been struggling to cope in jail. She wants her husband to bail her out, but after two weeks behind bars, its the women in her anger management class who are helping her most. Cameras follow her case at Western Massachusetts Regional Womens Correctional Centre. Kathy Burke narrates

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: The longest-serving inmate in Western Massachusetts Regional Womens Correctional Centre faces another 11 years in prison for manslaughter, and struggles to come to terms with what she has done. A stripper convicted of ID fraud who gave birth in prison tries to turn her life around with a training programme, and a violent prisoner is being moved out of segregation and into a shared tank with seven others

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some peoples most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team of experts make a property in Cork wheelchair-friendly for Adam Drummond. The 22-year-old former Irish International basketball player is currently adjusting to life in a wheelchair, and the team sets out to adapt his parents house in order to allow Adam to live in his family home once again

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Loughborough to meet a family of five, whose identical twin babies have been diagnosed as having a form of dwarfism, while at the age of 41, dad Mark is recovering from aggressive bowel and liver cancer. Now they are struggling for space. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their home, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they dont stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Epsom to meet paramedics Sam and Ben Laws, who have two children, one with special needs. The couple already find it hard, working opposite shifts which mean they often dont see each other for days on end - and to make matters worse, they have found their house to be inadequate for their requirements. After a close friend nominates them for the DIY SOS treatment, Nick and the team - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - turn up to transform it into a home they can enjoy as a family, now and in the future

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Holmfirth, West Yorkshire, to meet Richard Ford, a former policeman who suffered a devastating brain-stem stroke in 2012, and is now confined to an electric wheelchair. With limited movement and speech, he communicates with wife Jude and his three sons via an iPad, but has little privacy when it comes to using the toilet. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team and designer Charlie Luxton turn up to transform the familys home, planning to give Richard the privacy he needs while also assisting his recovery

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A skier falls over on a steep slope and suffers a suspected brain injury in Wakefield and a DIY enthusiasts attempts to fix the heating in his chilly home end in disaster

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: True crime mini-series. Two serial killers operating separately from each other terrorise Baton Rouge and its surrounding area in Louisiana in the USA over the span of a decade. A series of brutal murders from 1992 to 2002 were linked by DNA evidence, and revealed a serial killer in Baton Rouge with a pattern of stalking and escalating violence

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: Investigators focused on a man in a white pickup, eventually identifying Derrick Todd Lee. His arrest finally ended the fear that had grown during his long, violent killing spree

programme.title: Tortured to Death: Murdering the Nanny
programme.desc: Documentary examining the murder of a young French nanny, 21-year-old Sophie Lionnet, whose body was then burned in the back garden of a south London home. Featuring interviews with friends, neighbours and Old Bailey reporters

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Shoplifter and addict Linda is already planning her next big heist - stealing the ingredients for a celebratory feast when she gets out. But when jail life becomes depressingly familiar, she is forced to accept that confronting her demons may be the only way to break her cycle of addiction and crime

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part One
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team take on their most ambitious project to date and transform a whole street of run-down empty properties in Manchester into homes for veterans. They are joined by the local community, as well as Prince William and Prince Harry, who volunteer their assistance on site. In the first edition of the two-part special, the team builds houses for two former soldiers who fought in Iraq and Afghanistan and are suffering from post traumatic stress disorder. However, with the number of volunteers lacking and the buildings proving to be trickier than expected, the build is becoming the toughest yet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part Two
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team continue with their most ambitious project to date, with the assistance of the local community and two royal helpers, Prince William and Prince Harry. The second edition sees them create a home for a veteran, who is a triple amputee after an IED blew up in Afghanistan. They are also aiming to renovate the fronts of the houses on the street and put in new utilities, as well as creating a lasting legacy in the form of a support centre, which will help other veterans find homes and jobs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team heads to Birmingham to transform the family home of parents Charlotte and Chris, whose twins were born prematurely and have a condition known as global development delay. It has slowed down their progress of walking and talking, but they are further hindered by their cramped house. Nick Knowles and the team join forces with a host of volunteers from the local community to rebuild the familys life and give them a better future

programme.desc: Drew Scott and Jonathan Scott help a woman looking for a fresh start to find a modern property as her new home

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Property experts Drew Scott and Jonathan Scott help couple Dereck and Christine trade in their small urban condo for a roomier, detached home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part One
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team take on their most ambitious project to date and transform a whole street of run-down empty properties in Manchester into homes for veterans. They are joined by the local community, as well as Prince William and Prince Harry, who volunteer their assistance on site. In the first edition of the two-part special, the team builds houses for two former soldiers who fought in Iraq and Afghanistan and are suffering from post traumatic stress disorder. However, with the number of volunteers lacking and the buildings proving to be trickier than expected, the build is becoming the toughest yet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part Two
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team continue with their most ambitious project to date, with the assistance of the local community and two royal helpers, Prince William and Prince Harry. The second edition sees them create a home for a veteran, who is a triple amputee after an IED blew up in Afghanistan. They are also aiming to renovate the fronts of the houses on the street and put in new utilities, as well as creating a lasting legacy in the form of a support centre, which will help other veterans find homes and jobs

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker as DI Annika Strandhed, who has returned to Glasgow to head up the Marine Homicide Unit, where she is tasked with solving puzzling crimes and unexplained murders that wash up in Scotlands waters. On her first day in a new role, Annika is called in to solve the murder of a man found in the Clyde, with a harpoon piercing his head

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When a body is found on a boat in the Clyde, the case takes the team to the Isle of Bute. Evidence points to a jilted lover, but a darker secret emerges as the team investigate. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Jamie Sives and Katie Leung

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Follow real-time shifts of newly-qualified doctors at the Royal Berkshire Hospital in Reading. Its induction day for 26 junior doctors as they head to the Emergency Department

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Documentary series where the TV presenter uncovers what life is like for women behind bars in the United States at the New Orleans Parish Prison. As she meets the inmates, Katie delves into inmates cases and tries to uncover what drove these women to commit the crimes that they have been accused of.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before its too late. The trio help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels, let go of the past and move forward

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Shoplifter and addict Linda is already planning her next big heist - stealing the ingredients for a celebratory feast when she gets out. But when jail life becomes depressingly familiar, she is forced to accept that confronting her demons may be the only way to break her cycle of addiction and crime

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some peoples most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team of experts make a property in Cork wheelchair-friendly for Adam Drummond. The 22-year-old former Irish International basketball player is currently adjusting to life in a wheelchair, and the team sets out to adapt his parents house in order to allow Adam to live in his family home once again

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Loughborough to meet a family of five, whose identical twin babies have been diagnosed as having a form of dwarfism, while at the age of 41, dad Mark is recovering from aggressive bowel and liver cancer. Now they are struggling for space. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their home, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they dont stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Epsom to meet paramedics Sam and Ben Laws, who have two children, one with special needs. The couple already find it hard, working opposite shifts which mean they often dont see each other for days on end - and to make matters worse, they have found their house to be inadequate for their requirements. After a close friend nominates them for the DIY SOS treatment, Nick and the team - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - turn up to transform it into a home they can enjoy as a family, now and in the future

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Holmfirth, West Yorkshire, to meet Richard Ford, a former policeman who suffered a devastating brain-stem stroke in 2012, and is now confined to an electric wheelchair. With limited movement and speech, he communicates with wife Jude and his three sons via an iPad, but has little privacy when it comes to using the toilet. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team and designer Charlie Luxton turn up to transform the familys home, planning to give Richard the privacy he needs while also assisting his recovery

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: True crime mini-series. Two serial killers operating separately from each other terrorise Baton Rouge and its surrounding area in Louisiana in the USA over the span of a decade. A series of brutal murders from 1992 to 2002 were linked by DNA evidence, and revealed a serial killer in Baton Rouge with a pattern of stalking and escalating violence

programme.title: Butchers of the Bayou
programme.desc: Investigators focused on a man in a white pickup, eventually identifying Derrick Todd Lee. His arrest finally ended the fear that had grown during his long, violent killing spree

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Accused of defrauding an elderly person, Tonika has been struggling to cope in jail. She wants her husband to bail her out, but after two weeks behind bars, its the women in her anger management class who are helping her most. Cameras follow her case at Western Massachusetts Regional Womens Correctional Centre. Kathy Burke narrates

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Documentary series where the TV presenter uncovers what life is like for women behind bars in the United States at the New Orleans Parish Prison. As she meets the inmates, Katie delves into inmates cases and tries to uncover what drove these women to commit the crimes that they have been accused of.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries focused on families, including his trilogy of documentaries centred around the Westboro Baptist Church. He examines how the death of patriarch Fred Phelps has left members of the church divided. He also reflects on how physical and mental health or forces beyond control can threaten to derail family lives

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England in the inter-war period, starting in Canterbury, Kent, where he treads the boards as he uncovers the political message behind a 1936 play, inspired by the 12th-century murder of Archbishop Thomas a Becket. In Maidstone, he learns of the international origins of the most British symbol of remembrance. Outside Sevenoaks, Michael visits the country home of Winston Churchill, and discovers how the 1930s were wilderness years at Chartwell, as Churchill warned against Nazi appeasement. Once in the capital, he heads to Alexandra Palace, the birthplace of television

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England en route to Lincolnshire, discovering how the Crossrail project is helping to conserve birds and taking a gelato cart to Southend. Plus, Michael alights at Limehouse in east London for Cable Street

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos railway journey through 1930s Britain from Canterbury to Skegness reaches Witham in Essex, where he visits the factory of the worlds oldest supplier of metal-framed windows, which became popular in the Thirties. Crossing into Suffolk, he alights at Ipswich and discovers the story of a group of child refugees whose history is intertwined with that of Michaels family

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich. Plus, he learns how the music and life of one of Britains greatest composers was shaped by the sea and his Suffolk surroundings

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Austin Healeys are classic British vehicles that are difficult to tame. Paul and Charlie have the rare chance to pick up a trio of these iconic cars in varying conditions

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks got his work cut out collecting a huge haul that includes two Bentleys, a Daimler and a traditional Bowtop caravan

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitlers Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: To improve their bombs, the US Navy turned to animals for inspiration. However, when 270 men vanished into a cloud, witnesses claimed the soldiers had been abducted by aliens

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Portchester Castle on the Hampshire coast, where finds brought in include a previously unpublished poem by Siegfried Sassoon, a water-damaged letter that survived the sinking of the Titanic, a Victoria Cross medal, a rare cigarette lighter and a painting by one of the stars of the original Hawaii 5-0. Plus, Lee Young challenges Fiona to place a trio of model cars in order of value

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh. She gets to see one of the resident botanical artists at work capturing flowers and plants on paper, and finds out about Lillian Snelling, a pioneering artist from the early 20th century. Items brought in to be valued include two paintings of three-dimensional raindrops, a botanists gadget cane, and a curious rocking horse in the shape of a British army general

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team snap up a slightly saggy 1970s Saab 96, but despite a stunning restoration, could it result in a record financial loss when it goes to auction?

programme.title: Operation Arctic
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan visits Churchill, Manitoba, a Canadian town where humans live in close proximity to polar bears. For two months of every year, the animals gather in the area, waiting for sea ice to form so they can head north for their winter hunt. Gordon meets local police officers and refuse collectors who have to work in close proximity to the bears and meets a woman who was mauled on the towns main street

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A beautiful looking sports car has been fooling the punters and intriguing Derek. Its a Challenger E-Type - a fibreglass replica created from an E-Type mould

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team snap up a slightly saggy 1970s Saab 96, but despite a stunning restoration, could it result in a record financial loss when it goes to auction?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michaels journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAFs latest combat aircraft

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through Englands Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in north-west London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave has to extricate a VW Golf from a garden that has grown up around it

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is off to Bridlington to pick up a trio of classics - a Morris 8 tourer, a Mini Van and a 1940s Morris LC3 truck

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitlers ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leaders promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Crossword puzzles in a national newspaper begin to attract the attention of MI5 when they appear to reveal information about the upcoming D-Day landings

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England in the inter-war period, starting in Canterbury, Kent, where he treads the boards as he uncovers the political message behind a 1936 play, inspired by the 12th-century murder of Archbishop Thomas a Becket. In Maidstone, he learns of the international origins of the most British symbol of remembrance. Outside Sevenoaks, Michael visits the country home of Winston Churchill, and discovers how the 1930s were wilderness years at Chartwell, as Churchill warned against Nazi appeasement. Once in the capital, he heads to Alexandra Palace, the birthplace of television

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England en route to Lincolnshire, discovering how the Crossrail project is helping to conserve birds and taking a gelato cart to Southend. Plus, Michael alights at Limehouse in east London for Cable Street

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos railway journey through 1930s Britain from Canterbury to Skegness reaches Witham in Essex, where he visits the factory of the worlds oldest supplier of metal-framed windows, which became popular in the Thirties. Crossing into Suffolk, he alights at Ipswich and discovers the story of a group of child refugees whose history is intertwined with that of Michaels family

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich. Plus, he learns how the music and life of one of Britains greatest composers was shaped by the sea and his Suffolk surroundings

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Ghosts of Alderney: Hitlers Island Slaves
programme.desc: Artist Piers Secunda uncovers the hidden history of Alderney to reveal new evidence of atrocities committed by the Nazis on this tiny island during the Second World War. He examines how slave workers from across Europe were shipped to Alderney to build a fortress for Hitler

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: To improve their bombs, the US Navy turned to animals for inspiration. However, when 270 men vanished into a cloud, witnesses claimed the soldiers had been abducted by aliens

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Portchester Castle on the Hampshire coast, where finds brought in include a previously unpublished poem by Siegfried Sassoon, a water-damaged letter that survived the sinking of the Titanic, a Victoria Cross medal, a rare cigarette lighter and a painting by one of the stars of the original Hawaii 5-0. Plus, Lee Young challenges Fiona to place a trio of model cars in order of value

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh. She gets to see one of the resident botanical artists at work capturing flowers and plants on paper, and finds out about Lillian Snelling, a pioneering artist from the early 20th century. Items brought in to be valued include two paintings of three-dimensional raindrops, a botanists gadget cane, and a curious rocking horse in the shape of a British army general

programme.title: Operation Arctic
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan visits Churchill, Manitoba, a Canadian town where humans live in close proximity to polar bears. Gordon meets local police officers and refuse collectors who have to work in close proximity to the bears and meets a woman who was mauled on the towns main street

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michaels journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAFs latest combat aircraft

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through Englands Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to leafy Hatch End in north-west London, where he meets the great-nephew of the famous illustrator William Heath Robinson. Michael continues his journey through Slough and Stoke Poges before reaching Windsor, where he surveys the great walls of the castle, chief residence of the British monarch and the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world

programme.desc: A peek inside dirty and dismal dwellings, including Britains most insanitary student digs, which boast a bath coated in body fat, and a macabre zoo housing all manner of creatures. programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Time Is Money

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team are on a mission to prove to the nation that you can achieve the home renovation of your dreams without it literally costing the earth. Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as 2 households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week. Each family has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: A recent life-changing medical diagnosis has thrown psychologist Joe OLoughlin off-balance, and he is feeling reckless. When a young woman is found murdered, the unusual manner of her death prompts DS Riya Devi to suggest asking Joe for a professional opinion. Joe is shocked by the sight of the body - but is he just shaken by the brutality of the killing or is there something from his past hes not telling the police? Thriller, starring Aidan Turner, Shaun Parkes and Anjli Mohindra

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Lucy Kennedy heads to Sixmilebridge Co Clare, where GAA player Davy Fitzgerald lives with his partner and son. programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Cleanfluencer Brandon turns cleaning up into a tidy business, Tati and her crew tackle a filthy house and Just for Kicks revive some battered trainers

programme.title: The Girl Before

In flashback, Emma confides in Edward about her assault as she is increasingly uneasy about the way the way the police investigation is heading

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The detective and his team are called to the site of a fatal crash involving an articulated lorry, a car and a blue pick-up truck. An unexpected discovery found on the victim at the scene soon suggests there could be far more to this particular accident than meets the eye, sparking an unsettling chain of events that leads Grace and Branson on a game of cat and mouse through Brighton to save an innocent life before time runs out

programme.title: Cracker
programme.sub-title: One Day a Lemming Will Fly - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Fitz helps the police probe a youngsters disappearance, drawing up character profiles of those closest to him and suggesting how inquiries should be conducted. Unfortunately, his investigation provokes a frenzy of violence, ending in tragedy and forcing him to confront his own tortured personal life. Robbie Coltrane stars, with Barbara Flynn and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder tells the story of Frances Molloy, whose 18-year-old son was killed in a coach crash in 2012, leading her to fight for changes to the law, making it illegal for buses and coaches to use tyres that are more than 10 years old. He also examines the 1973 case of David McGreavy, who murdered three children he was supposed to be looking after

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Peggy hatches a plan to save Bernie when he becomes the target of a predatory widow (guest star Mel Martin). Carol struggles to control her feelings of guilt when a newborn baby dies in her care, and grows more distressed when Rob arrests the bereaved father after a violent altercation at the Aidensfield Arms. Thomas Craig also guests

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Old and new wishes start coming true after Cassie, Abigail and Joy toss coins into the historic wishing well. Catherine Bell stars

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the history of personal flight, culminating in Richard Browning and his team of engineers development of the Gravity Industries Jet Suit

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group begin their second week, having settled into their new home on the coast of Puducherry. Henry Blofeld is keen to find a cure for an old injury, so he and Paul Chuckle visit a local hospital where western medicine is combined with yoga. Paul also braves the chaotic roads as he throws himself headlong into his first Indian driving lesson, while Britt Ekland and Zandra Rhodes explore the more spiritual side of their temporary home. Later in the week, some of the group catch the sleeper train to Madurai, where they enjoy the wild celebrations of the famous Chithirai festival

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade responds to reports from one of the US east coasts most popular vacation destinations, the sunny Florida Keys. A creature is attacking both visitors and locals alike, striking its victims so violently it is slicing open flesh and even smashing bones, and the assaults are happening both above and below the water. Jeremy is determined to discover the identity of the assailant and uncover what is triggering these attacks. He knows it is not going to be easy, and it is not long before the intrepid marine biologist fears he may be out of his depth

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie heads to a friends farm where a young calf has developed a serious limp. The calfs protective mother is making it clear she wants her baby back, and Jennie does not want to distress her, so has to work fast. Vet Darren treats a two-day-old lamb, whose owner thinks has a urinary infection. However, when she happily relieves herself all over Darrens examination table, he has to use all his investigative skills to diagnose the real problem. Maurice King, the founder of the Downe Veterinary Clinic, encounters a loggerhead turtle has been rescued by the local sea life sanctuary

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Workers at a visiting funfair get their hands on a stolen painting and sell it to Greengrass. In characteristic fashion he sets out to make a quick return on his investment and wastes no time in trying to offload it on Dr Ferrenby - who realises its value and informs Nick. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help Daria and Emma slim down for a birthday bash in seven days, and cafe cook Stacie Stewart helps dieters turn their diets into delicious dishes. Plus, bridesmaids Emma and Elisa go on six week dieting plans for a forthcoming wedding, and entrepreneurs Dom and Adam want to slim down for a corporate summer party in four months. To find out how fasting affects body and mind, Xand also goes on an extreme water-only fast, and investigates if so called zero calorie and negative calorie foods really exist

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits Eden Lodge in the popular holiday and sailing resort of Falmouth in Cornwall, booking into the BandB as a guest to gain an impression of the average customer experience. She is not impressed by owners Lynn and Ziggys self-decorated bedrooms and the condition of their indoor swimming pool, and decides they need a serious wake-up call if they are to operate at a profit

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim may have been poisoned, while Paddy makes a mess of things and Grahams suspicions are aroused

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bens situation forces Tim to question his past, Gary sends Theo packing, George provides Todd with the means to make a quick getaway, and Bethany shows Jodie a picture of her date

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The X-ray machine breaks down, and Carnegie has to tell the hospital staff they wont be getting a replacement - meaning patients will have to be taken elsewhere. Dr Makori gets a crash course in dealing with the locals as she struggles to treat a complex ailment, and a window cleaner is injured while beating a hasty retreat from his married girlfriends bedroom

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patients miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victims seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead womans estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham

programme.title: Essex County
programme.desc: A story of loss and loneliness tracing the interconnected lives of two families in a rural community. Canadian drama, based on the graphic novel by Jeff Lemire

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: The submarine is forced into lockdown as disaster strikes. With hostile boats tracking and communications down, Vigils crew have to navigate this crisis alone. Kirsten desperately hunts for clues to reveal the source of the threat to Vigil, but with no word from the boat, she fears for the safety of Amy, whose quest to uncover the truth places her in grave danger

programme.title: The Ipcress File
programme.desc: In 1962 Berlin, British Army corporal Harry Palmer is released from military prison at the behest of unorthodox spymaster Dalby, who wants him to help find an abducted scientist critical to the creation of a new bomb to scare the Soviet Union. Cold War spy thriller adapted by John Hodge from the Len Deighton novel, starring Joe Cole, with Lucy Boynton and Tom Hollander

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Judge Rinder rules the courtroom in his hit series, but now the respected barrister with a criminal defence background steps out from behind the bench to take on real cases.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Phil and Geoff keep watch on the illegal gambling club owned by Kenny Dekin (guest Ron Donachie), in the hope of finding evidence to close the establishment. Rob is concerned about his future with Carol as he anxiously awaits the results of his sergeant exams, while David is pleased with the outcome when he strikes out on his own

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: As the Merriwicks delve into the images on Joys vision board, Cassie accompanies Sam on a trip to his childhood home to care for his sister. Catherine Bell stars

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into a team of engineers in Perths attempts to not only break the 763mph land speed record set back in 1997, but also go past the 1,000mph barrier

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The friends go all out to make Susies birthday a night to remember, and as the celebration begin, Britt is keen to show she still has what it takes by showing off her singing and dancing talents. The senior citizens also head to Chennai, where Zandra is reunited with a friend from 40 years ago and Henry relives some difficult memories of his last overseas broadcast at the citys famous cricket ground. Back in Puducherry, John and Duncan test their fitness by jumping on their bikes, while Paul, revelling in the chaos of the Indian roads, decides to take his mock Indian driving test

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade monster cases usually involve freshwater culprits, after hearing multiple reports of a deep-water serial killer in Mexicos Sea of Cortez, he decides to step out of his comfort zone and into unchartered depths. The extreme angler meets an attack survivor whose chilling account identifies the underwater assassin as el diablo rojo - the red devil, prompting Jeremy to brave sea-sickening conditions to track down a horrific beast, apparently even more fearsome than sharks

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnies labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates a break-in at the surgery, while Kate discovers the cause of a patients miscarriages and Greengrass claims his car has been stolen. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Cameras follow Stefanie and Jade from Harrow who have 10 days to slim down before the launch of their clothing label. There is also advice for fast food junkies Matt from Redditch and Robb from the Wirral. They battle the bulge before a university reunion in six weeks time. Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie also follow the progress of Vicki and Kerry from Chesterfield, who want to get fighting fit for a mud race in four months

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Best friends Neil and John took over the Vidella Hotel in Blackpool a decade ago, but their love of practical jokes is beginning to wear thin and falling trade has started to take its toll on them. Alex Polizzi tries to turn their struggling business around, but has her work cut out to prove to the owners that a failing hotel is not a laughing matter

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headline stories from 2000 to a soundtrack of contemporary music

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2011

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A patient who set fire to his own shop (guest star Tony Slattery) attempts to avoid police questioning by leaping into an ambulance and driving off towards the pier - unaware Alun and a sick man are in the back of the vehicle. Meanwhile, Dr Makori fears for her relationship with Dr Fox when he tells her he has been offered a job in Biafra. Medical drama, with Andy Wear, Paul Fox and Kananu Kirimi

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick awaits the arrival in Aidensfield of a violent criminal recently released from prison, and Kate finds herself cut off by snow when she tries to help a heavily pregnant patient get to hospital. Meanwhile, Greengrass braves the harsh weather himself when his faithful canine companion Alfred goes missing. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The death of an ancient estates patriarch leads to rivalry and murder when his rebellious son plans to sell it off, outraging his siblings. Detective drama set in a picturesque rural county where death lurks around every corner

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A vengeful Maggie pays Megan a visit at her flat, Lou comes to the rescue when Debbie picks a fight, George despairs as Adam and Alya build the case against him, and Bethany confides in Jodie about being stood up

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain feels he has been betrayed, while Joe is annoyed and Marlon is put out

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry is shipped back to Manchester due to concerns about his mental health, and is furious at the implication he is doomed to follow in his fathers footsteps. As he returns home, Kasia prepares to leave for Europe on an important mission. Henriette returns home have smuggled David out of France, and Marga plots to flee for Berlin. Rajib encounters a German patrol and tries to negotiate a ceasefire

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: When Jackie goes to treatment for her eating disorder and receives life-threatening news, Dolores plots how to get back at her for questioning her loyalty

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Naisha finds the double pressure of a health app and her parents too much to take, Jonny and Bexs romance gets off to a rocky start, Dylan is made to feel like an unwelcome house guest, and Ella worries that her impulsive behaviour towards Josh is going to have serious consequences

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Ed, Sheila, Ciara and Jas vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: As the team at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza prepare to throw a party to show off the hotel to local travel agents, the Spanish resort is hit by a rare rainstorm, resulting in the hotel springing leaks and causing the electricity to cut out. Meanwhile, a 14-strong friendship group party in memory of their dear friend, and a couple celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary at Benidorms famously romantic waterfalls

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. This time, Security Officer Estefania investigates an oddly shaped object, and Passenger Experience Manager Harry deals with a medical emergency. Elsewhere, Border Force Bob helps a circus performer with visa troubles

programme.title: Live UEFA European Qualifiers
programme.desc: Czechia v Denmark (Kick-off 7.45pm). Coverage of the FIFA World Cup qualifying play-off final at EPET Arena in Prague. These teams defeated Republic of Ireland and North Macedonia respectively in the semi-finals and now compete for a place in this summers finals

programme.title: Bargain Fever Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. A look at how the four discount retailers fare during the sales season. At Approved Food in Sheffield, manager Dan tries to capitalise on the Black Friday chaos, tempting shoppers with a host of penny deals on the website. At Motorpoint in Newport, Russ and his team of salesmen struggle to meet targets in December - the worst time of year for the car trade - while the Christmas rush at American wholesale giant Costco causes trouble for turkey supervisor Tom. In Bradford, the Shoe Zone team has just 45 minutes to get all the Boxing Day promotions ready. Narrated by Jill Halfpenny
